name: Torah Literary Units Dataset
description: The 86 literary units of the Torah (Five Books of Moses) as identified by the Woven Torah hypothesis. Each unit is a self-contained two-dimensional literary composition with rows and columns. The cells_detail field maps each cell label to its verse range, enabling reconstruction of the woven reading using any translation. Published in the Journal of Biblical Literature (2025).
author: Moshe Kline
citation: Kline, Moshe. Before Chapter and Verse: Reading the Woven Torah. Self-published, 2022.

format_key.NxM: Regular unit: N rows by M columns (same number of columns per row)
format_key.digit_string: Irregular unit: each digit represents the number of columns in that row (e.g., 12221 = rows of width 1,2,2,2,1). All irregular units are symmetrical.

type_key.F: Framework unit — marks structural boundaries within the book
type_key.U: Unique — not grouped in a triadic pattern with other units
